info.name: Nexus 2.0 API
info.description: Comprehensive API collection for the Nexus 2.0 Enterprise Agile Platform covering all 5 microservices: SecurityService, ProfileService, WorkService, UtilityService, and BillingService.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints: login, logout, token refresh, OTP

request.description: Authenticate a user with email and password. Returns JWT access token, refresh token, and expiry. The test script automatically extracts tokens into environment variables.
Rotate the refresh token and obtain a new access token. Uses refresh token rotation — the old refresh token is invalidated.
Logout the current session. Blacklists the current JWT and removes the session from Redis.
Request a 6-digit OTP for the given identity (email). OTP is valid for 5 minutes with max 3 verification attempts.
Verify a 6-digit OTP code for the given identity.

item.name: Password
item.description: Password management: change, reset request, reset confirm

request.description: Change the current user's password. Required for first-time users. Password must meet complexity requirements: 8+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character.
Request a password reset OTP for the given email address. The OTP is sent to the user's email.
Confirm a password reset using the OTP code and set a new password. Password must meet complexity requirements.

item.name: Sessions
item.description: Session management: list, revoke single, revoke all

request.description: List all active sessions for the current user. Returns device ID, IP address, and creation timestamp for each session.
Revoke a specific session by session ID. The session's JWT is blacklisted.
Revoke all sessions except the current one. Useful for security incidents or when a user suspects unauthorized access.
Issue a service-to-service JWT token. Used by other microservices to authenticate inter-service calls. Requires service auth.

item.name: ProfileService
item.description: Identity, organization, and profile management microservice (port 5002)

request.description: Get organization details by ID. Returns organization name, settings, story ID prefix, and status.
Update organization settings including sprint duration, story point scale, default board view, and auto-assignment strategy. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Create a new organization. Seeds 5 default departments (ENG, QA, DEVOPS, PROD, DESIGN). Requires PlatformAdmin or OrgAdmin role.
Provision an OrgAdmin for an organization. Creates the team member, assigns OrgAdmin role, generates credentials via SecurityService. Requires PlatformAdmin role.
List team members in the current organization. Supports filtering by departmentId, role, status, and availability.
Get detailed team member profile including departments, roles, professional ID, and availability status.
Update a team member's profile information.
List all departments in the current organization.
Create a new department in the current organization. Department code must be unique within the organization.

item.name: Invites
item.description: Invitation system for onboarding new team members

request.description: List pending invitations for the current organization. OrgAdmins see all invites; DeptLeads see invites for their department.
Create a new invitation. Generates a cryptographic token with 48-hour expiry. The invitee receives an email with the invite link.
List all registered devices for the current user. Maximum 5 devices per user.
Get the current user's preferences (theme, date format, time format, digest frequency, etc.).
Update the current user's preferences.
Get notification settings for the current user across all notification types and channels (Email, Push, InApp).
Get the navigation menu structure for the current user based on their role and permissions.
List all projects in the current organization. Supports filtering by status and pagination.
Create a new project with a unique project key (2-10 uppercase alphanumeric). The project key is used as a prefix for story IDs (e.g., MOB-1, MOB-2). Requires DeptLead role.
Get project details by ID including story count, sprint count, and project settings.

item.name: Stories
item.description: Story CRUD, status transitions, and assignment

request.description: List stories in the current organization. Supports filtering by projectId, status, priority, departmentId, assigneeId, sprintId, labels, dateFrom, and dateTo.

body.raw:
{
    "projectId": "{{project_id}}",
    "title": "User login flow",
    "description": "Implement the user login flow with email and password",
    "priority": "High",
    "storyPoints": 5
}

request.description: Create a new story in a project. Automatically generates a professional story ID (e.g., MOB-42) using an atomic per-project sequence. Story starts in Backlog status.
Get story details by ID including tasks, labels, links, assignee, and activity log.
Transition a story's status. Valid transitions follow the workflow state machine: Backlog → Ready → InProgress → InReview → QA → Done → Closed.
Create a new task under a story. Task type determines department auto-routing (Development → ENG, Testing → QA, etc.). Task starts in ToDo status.
List sprints in the current organization. Supports filtering by status and projectId.
Create a new sprint for a project. Sprint starts in Planning status. Only one active sprint per project is allowed. Requires DeptLead role.
Start a sprint (transition from Planning to Active). Only one active sprint per project is allowed. Requires DeptLead role.
Complete a sprint. Calculates velocity (sum of Done/Closed story points), moves incomplete stories back to Backlog. Requires DeptLead role.
Get the Kanban board view with stories grouped by status columns. Supports optional projectId, sprintId, departmentId, assigneeId, priority, and labels filters.
Get the Sprint board view showing the active sprint's stories and progress. Supports optional projectId filter.
Create a comment on a story or task. Supports @mentions which trigger notifications to mentioned users.

item.name: Search
item.description: Full-text search across stories and tasks

request.description: Full-text search across stories and tasks using PostgreSQL tsvector/tsquery with weighted search. Minimum 2 characters required.
Get velocity chart data for the last N completed sprints. Shows story points completed per sprint.
Query audit logs for the current organization. Supports filtering by serviceName, action, entityType, userId, dateFrom, and dateTo. Audit logs are immutable — no update or delete.
Get notification history for the current user. Supports filtering by notificationType, channel, status, dateFrom, and dateTo.

item.name: Reference Data
item.description: Platform reference data (department types, priorities, task types, workflow states)

request.description: Get all department types. No authentication required. Cached in Redis with 24-hour TTL.
Get all priority levels. No authentication required.
Get all task type references. No authentication required.
Get all workflow states for stories and tasks. No authentication required.
List all registered error codes in the platform. Cached in Redis with 24-hour TTL.

item.name: BillingService
item.description: Subscription management, plan tiers, feature gating, and usage tracking (port 5300)

request.description: List all active subscription plans (Free, Starter, Professional, Enterprise) with pricing, feature limits, and tier levels.
Get the current organization's subscription details including plan info, usage metrics, and billing period. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Create a new subscription for the organization. Paid plans start with a 14-day trial. Free plan activates immediately. One subscription per organization. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Upgrade to a higher-tier plan. Prorated charges apply immediately. If upgrading during trial, trial ends and paid billing starts. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Schedule a downgrade to a lower-tier plan at the end of the current billing period. Blocked if current usage exceeds target plan limits. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Cancel the current subscription. Effective at the end of the current billing period. Free plan cancellation is immediate. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Get the current organization's usage metrics (active_members, stories_created, storage_bytes) with current values, limits, and percentage utilization. Requires OrgAdmin role.
Check if a feature is allowed for the given organization based on their plan limits. Returns allowed status, current usage, and limit. Used for service-to-service feature gating.
